tools.name: AdFind
tools.evidence_snippet: A command-line tool used to query and retrieve information from Active Directory [T1087.002 <https://attack.mitre.org/versions/v18/techniques/t1087/002/>].

tools.name: AnyDesk
tools.evidence_snippet: Legitimate desktop support software used to obtain remote access to victim systems and for lateral movement, and support remote file transfer.

tools.name: LaZagne
tools.evidence_snippet: An open source tool that enables users to recover stored passwords on Windows, Linux, and Mac OS X systems [T1555 <https://attack.mitre.org/versions/v18/techniques/t1555/>].

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.evidence_snippet: A credential dumper that extracts plaintext credentials from memory. It can be used to obtain login information for Windows systems [T1003 <https://attack.mitre.org/versions/v18/techniques/t1003/>].

cves.evidence_snippet: CVE-2020-3259 [Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE)-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor <https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/200.html>];
CVE-2023-20269 [CWE-288: Authentication Bypass Using an Alternate Path or Channel <https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/288.html>];

CVE-2020-3580 [CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (‘Cross-site Scripting’) <https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/79.html>]  </news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-109a#note3>
CVE-2024-37085 [CWE-305: Authentication Bypass by Primary Weakness <https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/305.html>].  </news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-109a#note5>
CVE-2023-27532 [CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function <https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/306.html>];
